assertions.title: Successful submission creates a sibling worktree from the repo default branch

steps.expected: Function creates worktree using git worktree add -b with default branch as base
steps.observed: Implementation uses `git worktree add -b ${finalBranch} ${finalPath} ${defaultBranch}` where defaultBranch is passed from repo context

steps.expected: deriveSiblingPath creates path sibling to repo root
steps.observed: Implementation extracts parent directory from repoRoot and creates worktree as sibling

steps.expected: Worktree created from main branch in sibling directory
steps.observed: Created /tmp/rudu-test-worktree from main branch; git worktree list shows worktree with correct branch reference

steps.action: Run bun test for worktree service tests
steps.expected: All tests pass including branch/path derivation tests
steps.observed: 155 tests passed including deriveBranchName, deriveSiblingPath, and previewWorktreeNames tests

evidence.implementation: src/services/worktree/GitWorktreeService.ts lines 125-135 show git worktree add with defaultBranch parameter
evidence.testOutput: GitWorktreeService.test.ts: deriveBranchName, deriveSiblingPath, previewWorktreeNames tests all pass
evidence.manualVerification: Created worktree at /tmp/rudu-test-worktree from main branch with rudu/test-worktree branch

assertions.title: Successful worktree creation automatically creates the first session inside that worktree

steps.expected: After successful worktree creation, immediately creates a session with worktree path as cwd
steps.observed: Implementation calls sessionManager.queuePiSession with cwd: result.worktree.path and metadata.worktreeId

steps.action: Verify session metadata includes worktree linkage
steps.observed: metadata: { worktreeId: result.worktree.id } is passed when creating session

steps.action: Verify session runs in worktree directory
steps.expected: Session cwd is set to worktree path
steps.observed: cwd: result.worktree.path ensures session runs in worktree directory
SessionManager.worktree.test.ts verifies sessions can be created with worktreeId and rehydrated with linkage

evidence.implementation: src/app/App.tsx lines 109-120 show session creation immediately after successful worktree creation
evidence.testOutput: SessionManager.worktree.test.ts passes - sessions with worktreeId are properly persisted and rehydrated

assertions.title: Create-flow failures are visible and recoverable

steps.action: Verify error handling in createWorktree function
steps.expected: Returns failure result with recoverable flag
steps.observed: CreateWorktreeFailure type includes error and recoverable boolean; try-catch handles git errors

steps.expected: Common errors (already exists, permission denied, etc.) marked as recoverable
steps.observed: recoverable flag set based on error message containing 'already exists', 'is already checked out', 'not a git repository', 'permission denied', 'could not create directory'

steps.expected: createError state set on failure; dialog stays open
steps.observed: handleSubmitCreateDialog sets createError on failure result; does not close dialog so user can retry

steps.expected: Creation errors displayed in dialog UI
steps.observed: CreateWorktreeDialog accepts error prop and displays it with showCreationError conditional rendering

steps.action: Verify validation errors also block submission
steps.expected: Invalid titles blocked with validation error
steps.observed: validateTitle function checks length and format; validationError displayed in UI

implementation: src/services/worktree/GitWorktreeService.ts lines 165-182 show error handling with recoverable flag
src/app/App.tsx lines 102-108 show error state handling on failure
src/components/CreateWorktreeDialog.tsx lines 75-80 and 103-108 show error display

summary: All 3 assertions passed. Implementation correctly: (1) creates sibling worktrees from default branch with collision handling, (2) automatically creates linked sessions in new worktrees with proper worktreeId metadata, (3) displays recoverable errors in UI without closing dialog, allowing retry. Tests pass (155 total) including specific worktree linkage and derivation tests.
